purpose: Machine-optimized summary router that helps an AI decide which EAFIX/HUEY_P project knowledge files to open for a subject, module, step, contract, service, communication channel, UI screen, capability, source file, conflict, or workflow question without opening every project file.

routing_basis.route_applied_for_this_file: documentation/reference-summary creation; merged module architecture, process lifecycle, service reuse, MT4 bridge, UI, calendar, and source-inventory routes.
governing_conflict_rules_summary: Subject-specific canonical JSON or active governance baseline wins over narrative notes, historical Markdown, extracted PDF text, and implementation samples.
MT4 platform behavior, native feature availability, broker variability, and native data authority are governed by DOC_MT4_AUTHORITATIVE_REF.
Module identity, module symbols, module responsibilities, dependencies, and interfaces are governed by DOC_MODULE_CATALOG.

Process step IDs, step codes, handoff contracts, and dependency_step_ids are governed by DOC_PROCESS_STEP_CATALOG. Phase grouping and validation/failure contract IDs are governed by DOC_ALIGNED_PROCESS.
MT4-Python channel IDs, ports, protocols, directions, owning modules, and data contracts are governed by DOC_COMM_CHANNELS.
Operator UI products, screens, controls, REST/WebSocket contracts, disabled-state rules, safety levels, and acceptance criteria are governed by DOC_UI_CATALOG.
Calendar deterministic decisions, CAL8/CAL5/RCI identity, anti-patterns, and single-writer calendar authority are governed by DOC_CAL_DES_RULES.
Physical file-to-module mapping is governed by DOC_FILE_MODULE_MAPPING, but file mapping does not override canonical module/process catalogs.
Generated/session-derived service references and implementation sketches must be verified against current source before destructive changes.
Implementation samples, especially DOC_EA_EXECUTION_ENGINE_SAMPLE, are lowest authority and must not override canonical contracts or platform references.

reference_documents_from_routing.path: EAFIX_auth_docs/05_calendar_system/Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf

authority_model.default_rule: Open the most specific canonical structured file first; use narrative/PDF/session-derived files only for explanation, decomposition detail, or implementation context after canonical sources are checked.

lookup_algorithm: Classify the question by subject, module, process step, phase, contract, communication channel, UI screen, service, capability, source file, or conflict.
Check the matching index in this router.
Open only primary_files unless the question crosses boundaries or the primary file does not answer it.
Open companion_files when a task crosses module/process/contract/UI/MT4/calendar/service boundaries.
If a required reference is unavailable, state that clearly instead of guessing.
global_lookup_strategy.do_not_open_everything_rule: This router exists to narrow the reference set. Do not open all project files by default.

source_files.summary_short: Canonical module catalog for HUEY_P/EAFIX module identity, responsibility, interfaces, dependencies, process bindings, and quality gates.
not_authoritative_for: physical file ownership for every source file
open_when: Question mentions module ownership, module_id, canonical_symbol, module boundaries, scope_in/scope_out, dependency, interface, quality gate, or which module owns a behavior.

source_files.summary_short: Canonical 26-step process catalog mapping S01-S26 to modules, handoff contracts, dependency step IDs, validation rules, failure behavior, and entrypoint hints.
open_when: Question asks about handoffs, upstream/downstream dependencies, what produces or consumes a contract, process step order, validation, or failure behavior.

source_files.summary_short: Current aligned v2 process representation with 10 phases, 26 steps, phase module sequences, validation_contract IDs, and failure_contract IDs.
open_when: Question asks about phases, current aligned process, module sequence, validation contract IDs, failure contract IDs, or phase roadmap.

source_files.summary_short: Canonical MT4↔Python and internal communication channel catalog covering HTTP REST, DDE, JSONL outbox, CSV polling, Redis Pub/Sub, and TCP socket fallback.
open_when: Question mentions MT4-Python bridge, DDE, WebRequest, HTTP REST, port 5001, JSONL, CSV polling, Redis topics, socket fallback, or channel data contracts.

source_files.summary_short: Single source of truth for intended operator UI products, screens, panels, controls, filters, REST/WebSocket contracts, data dependencies, safety model, disabled-state rules, implementation bindings, and acceptance criteria.
not_authoritative_for: trading strategy calculations outside UI display/control
open_when: Question asks about dashboard, GUI, screen, tab, panel, control, REST API, WebSocket, operator action, safety state, disabled-state rule, or UI-visible data field.

source_files.summary_short: Governance baseline for MT4 platform facts, native capabilities, limitations, broker variability, MQL4 functions, desktop/web-terminal constraints, and custom-vs-native decisions.
open_when: Question mentions MT4, MetaTrader, MQL4, EA, WebRequest, DDE, DLL imports, OrderSend, broker constraints, native terminal data, Strategy Tester, desktop terminal, or web terminal.

source_files.summary_short: Human-readable architecture map grouping modules by platform, data ingest, compute/features, signal/intent, risk, OMS, MT4 bridge, reentry, UI/gateway, EA-side systems, shared libraries, and infrastructure.
not_authoritative_for: exact canonical module count when conflicting
open_when: Question asks for architectural overview, module groups, service homes, ports, EA-side decomposition, shared libraries, or a human-readable map.

source_files.summary_short: Physical file-to-module mapping CSV with service, role, canonical modules, and test flag for implementation files; incomplete and contains stale symbols.
not_authoritative_for: complete file ownership because many rows are unassigned
open_when: Question asks which physical files are associated with a module, service, implementation role, or process step.

source_files.summary_short: AI-oriented inventory of Python services, repository context, service architecture patterns, stable import wrappers, and service purposes.
not_authoritative_for: live current source truth without verification
open_when: Question asks what services exist, what a service does, service naming conventions, wrappers, or service reuse context.

source_files.summary_short: Capability registry for internal services, including cost model, best/poor use cases, provided functions, integration methods, and do-not-rebuild guidance.
open_when: Question asks whether an existing service/capability can replace a custom solution, whether something is free/internal, or how to avoid reinventing functionality.

source_files.summary_short: Mandatory routing authority telling AI which project knowledge documents to consult by subject, module, process step, channel, UI screen, contract, capability, service, and task type.
open_when: Before any project-related analysis, planning, coding, debugging, refactoring, validation, or modification.

source_files.summary_short: Prior AI reference catalog describing module-centric uploaded files, lookup order, conflicts, validation results, and older module/registry relationships.
authoritative_for: historical summary of older uploaded module-centric files
known drift in older symbolic-vs-numeric module identity models
open_when: Question asks about older module-centric file summaries, schema drift, or previous symbolic-ID catalog state.

source_files.summary_short: Legacy 11-phase / 71-step atomic runbook decomposing calendar intake, triggering, signal generation, risk, MT transport, EA execution, result capture, outcome classification, reentry, and reentry execution.
open_when: Question asks for fine-grained operational detail, older ATONIC steps, or comparison to canonical 26-step process.

source_files.summary_short: Explains why the 71-step ATONIC lifecycle and 26-step canonical process diverge, including decomposition-axis differences, reentry loop unrolling, and scope gaps.
not_authoritative_for: final crosswalk authority if CSV differs
open_when: Question asks why step counts differ, how ATONIC maps to canonical, or whether ATONIC is a superset.

source_files.summary_short: CSV crosswalk table mapping ATONIC operations to canonical process steps where possible.
authoritative_for: machine-readable ATONIC-to-canonical mapping rows where present
open_when: Question asks for row-level mapping between ATONIC steps and canonical steps.

source_files.summary_short: Module reconciliation worksheet comparing canonical module catalog, process catalog, aligned phases, module map, file mappings, checklist/submodule docs, communication channels, and services reference.
not_authoritative_for: current source truth beyond referenced inventories
open_when: Question asks what module files are missing, what modules are undocumented, which references drift, or what reconciliation flags exist.

source_files.summary_short: Machine-readable module reconciliation worksheet with module IDs, symbols, kinds, layers, map group, step, phase, service home, candidate service, owned channels, file counts, stale symbols, submodule docs, and drift flags.
open_when: Question asks for tabular module reconciliation, drift flags, or counts by module.

source_files.summary_short: Prior consolidated narrative summary of project knowledge files, authority tiers, extra repo files, missing files, and drift notes.
not_authoritative_for: canonical facts where source files exist
open_when: Question asks how prior file summaries were created or what extra repository/project-reference files were identified.

source_files.summary_short: Narrative breakdown of MT4↔Python communication channels, associated files, and canonical bridge flow.
open_when: Question asks for files associated with MT4↔Python communication or a readable bridge explanation.

source_files.summary_short: Clean compilable MQL4 sample EA implementing a simple moving-average crossover and proper OnInit/OnTick/OnDeinit structure; explicitly not the full canonical EAFIX execution engine.
open_when: Question asks about the sample EA code specifically or needs MQL4 syntax examples, not canonical trading-system behavior.

source_files.summary_short: Visual reference for calendar subsystem dependency layers, from CAL8 identity and SQLite registry through signal assembly, consumers, and feedback.
authoritative_for: visual orientation of calendar dependency layers
open_when: Question asks for calendar layer diagram or dependency layer orientation.

source_files.summary_short: Authoritative deterministic design rules for economic calendar architecture, CAL8/CAL5/RCI identity, state machine, debounce, single-writer authority, proximity, and anti-pattern guards.
open_when: Question asks about economic calendar, CAL8, CAL5, RCI, ForexFactory ingestion, proximity, calendar CSV, event state machine, debounce, file_seq, or checksum.

project_knowledge_routing.description: How AI decides which project knowledge file to open for a task.

module_architecture.description: Canonical module identity, ownership, responsibilities, dependencies, interfaces, service homes, and reconciliation.

process_lifecycle.description: Canonical process steps, phases, handoffs, validations, failures, ATONIC legacy detail, and step crosswalks.

economic_calendar.description: Calendar sourcing, ForexFactory ingestion, normalization, CAL8/CAL5/RCI, proximity, anticipation, state machine, CSV outputs, and calendar deployment references.
secondary_files: Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf
Economic Calendar Subsystem - Technical Analysis & Documentation.pdf
Enhanced Economic Calendar System - Complete Implementation.pdf

mt4_platform_and_mql4.description: MT4 platform facts, MQL4/EAs, native terminal capabilities, limitations, broker variability, and sample EA implementation.

mt4_python_bridge.description: MT4↔Python communication channels, protocols, ports, Redis topics, data contracts, and associated files.

operator_ui.description: Desktop operator UI, dashboard backend, GUI gateway, tabs, panels, controls, REST/WebSocket contracts, and UI safety controls.

service_reuse_and_capabilities.description: Internal service inventory, capability reuse, cost model, do-not-rebuild directives, and avoiding custom development where an existing service exists.

source_inventory_and_drift.description: Physical file inventory, module reconciliation, source-grounded implementation status, missing files, stale symbols, and conflict/drift reports.

validation_and_testing.description: Validation gates, test scaffolds, automation lifecycle verification, static integrity, graph connectivity, and test design.

F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES.purpose: Owns process step 1: Resolve configuration snapshot.

F3_CLOCK_SCHEDULER.purpose: Owns process step 2: Emit schedule tick (calendar polling cadence).

D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER.purpose: Owns process step 3: Poll calendar source(s).

D3_CALENDAR_NORMALIZER.purpose: Owns process step 4: Normalize raw calendar entries.

F2_EVENT_LOG.purpose: Owns process step 5: Persist calendar events (append-only).

D4_CALENDAR_TRIGGER_BUILDER.purpose: Owns process step 6: Build anticipation triggers from calendar.

D1_MARKET_FEED_ADAPTER.purpose: Owns process step 7: Ingest market ticks.

C1_BAR_BUILDER.purpose: Owns process step 8: Aggregate ticks into bars.

C3_FEATURE_PACKAGER.purpose: Owns process step 10: Assemble strategy feature frame.

S1_SIGNAL_ENGINE.purpose: Owns process step 11: Generate signal (or suppression).

S2_INTENT_BUILDER.purpose: Owns process step 12: Convert signal to trade intent.

R1_RISK_EVALUATOR.purpose: Owns process step 13: Evaluate risk and size.

R2_ORDER_INTENT_COMPILER.purpose: Owns process step 14: Compile order intent.

O1_ORDER_ROUTER.purpose: Owns process step 15: Route order to broker.

B1_MT4_ADAPTER_TRANSPORT.purpose: Owns process step 16: Serialize and send to MT4 adapter.

B2_MT4_EA_EXECUTOR.purpose: Owns process step 17: EA executes broker order.

B3_EXEC_EVENT_NORMALIZER.purpose: Owns process step 18: Normalize broker events to canonical reports.

O2_OMS_STATE_MACHINE.purpose: Owns process step 19: Apply execution reports to OMS state.

O3_TRADE_CLOSE_CLASSIFIER.purpose: Owns process step 20: Classify trade close and compute canonical PnL.

E2_PROXIMITY_EVALUATOR.purpose: Owns process step 22: Compute event proximity.

E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP.purpose: Owns process step 23: Lookup matrix decision.

E4_REENTRY_INTENT_BUILDER.purpose: Owns process step 24: Build reentry trade intent (or suppress).

F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR.purpose: Owns process step 25: Loop: reentry intent follows same risk->order->route->transport->execute chain.

P1_HEALTH_AGGREGATOR.purpose: Owns process step 26: Health aggregation + SLO evaluation.

SHARED_LIBS.purpose: Owns cross-cutting shared libraries used across canonical modules.

S01.validation: Schema validate; stable ordering; hash snapshot
S01.failure: Reject invalid config; continue using last known good snapshot

validation_contract.rule: Schema validate; stable ordering; hash snapshot

failure_contract.rule: Reject invalid config; continue using last known good snapshot

S02.failure: If scheduler stalls, emit HealthReport alert and pause polling

validation_contract.rule: Frozen-time mode supported for tests

failure_contract.rule: If scheduler stalls, emit HealthReport alert and pause polling

S03.failure: Retry w/ deterministic backoff; on persistent failure emit IngestError and skip cycle

validation_contract.rule: HTTP 200; payload parsable; source_event_id present

failure_contract.rule: Retry w/ deterministic backoff; on persistent failure emit IngestError and skip cycle

S04.validation: UTC conversion success; impact in enum; dedup policy applied
S04.failure: Emit NormalizationError for malformed rows; continue with valid CalendarEvent

validation_contract.rule: UTC conversion success; impact in enum; dedup policy applied

failure_contract.rule: Emit NormalizationError for malformed rows; continue with valid CalendarEvent

S05.validation: Idempotent by message_id; ordered by created_utc + tie-breaker
S05.failure: Rollback and retry once; on persistent failure halt calendar pipeline

validation_contract.rule: Idempotent by message_id; ordered by created_utc + tie-breaker

failure_contract.rule: Rollback and retry once; on persistent failure halt calendar pipeline

S06.validation: No duplicate trigger for event+offset; TTL rules applied
S06.failure: Default to conservative suppression metadata; emit error event

validation_contract.rule: No duplicate trigger for event+offset; TTL rules applied

failure_contract.rule: Default to conservative suppression metadata; emit error event

S07.validation: Timestamp freshness; symbol normalization; reject malformed

validation_contract.rule: Timestamp freshness; symbol normalization; reject malformed

failure_contract.rule: Emit IngestError; skip malformed ticks

S08.failure: Drop out-of-order ticks per policy; emit error event

validation_contract.rule: Deterministic bar boundaries; tie-breaker rules

failure_contract.rule: Drop out-of-order ticks per policy; emit error event

S09.validation: Deterministic rounding; indicator params pinned by config snapshot
S09.failure: Emit indicator error; do not output partial snapshots unless flagged

validation_contract.rule: Deterministic rounding; indicator params pinned by config snapshot

failure_contract.rule: Emit indicator error; do not output partial snapshots unless flagged

S10.validation: Exact declared feature set; stable ordering
S10.failure: Emit packaging error; do not emit FeatureFrame

validation_contract.rule: Exact declared feature set; stable ordering

failure_contract.rule: Emit packaging error; do not emit FeatureFrame

S11.validation: Suppression reasons enumerated; no sizing/broker specifics
S11.failure: On internal error, emit SignalSuppressed with deterministic reason code

validation_contract.rule: Suppression reasons enumerated; no sizing/broker specifics

failure_contract.rule: On internal error, emit SignalSuppressed with deterministic reason code

S12.validation: Stable correlation_id from Signal; constraints + rationale attached

validation_contract.rule: Stable correlation_id from Signal; constraints + rationale attached

failure_contract.rule: Reject intent; emit error event

S13.validation: Idempotent for same inputs; deterministic sizing + rounding
S13.failure: On rule engine failure default to REJECT with SAFETY_FAIL reason

validation_contract.rule: Idempotent for same inputs; deterministic sizing + rounding

failure_contract.rule: On rule engine failure default to REJECT with SAFETY_FAIL reason

S14.failure: If compilation fails, emit error; do not proceed to routing

validation_contract.rule: client_order_id + idempotency_key assigned per policy

failure_contract.rule: If compilation fails, emit error; do not proceed to routing

S15.failure: If routing fails, emit error and reject

validation_contract.rule: Routing deterministic; preserves OrderIntent hash

failure_contract.rule: If routing fails, emit error and reject

S16.step_name: Serialize and send to MT4 adapter
S16.validation: Atomic write+rename (no partial reads); sequence + hash; idempotent resend
S16.failure: Deterministic retry schedule; on persistent failure emit TransportError and stop

validation_contract.rule: Atomic write+rename (no partial reads); sequence + hash; idempotent resend

failure_contract.rule: Deterministic retry schedule; on persistent failure emit TransportError and stop

S17.validation: EA contains no strategy/risk logic; only execution
S17.failure: Classify and emit error events; retries per deterministic policy

validation_contract.rule: EA contains no strategy/risk logic; only execution

failure_contract.rule: Classify and emit error events; retries per deterministic policy

S18.step_name: Normalize broker events to canonical reports
S18.failure: Emit NormalizationError; quarantine malformed broker events

validation_contract.rule: Dedup + ordering policy; canonical rounding

failure_contract.rule: Emit NormalizationError; quarantine malformed broker events

S19.step_name: Apply execution reports to OMS state
S19.validation: Idempotent fills; out-of-order handling per policy; stable final state
S19.failure: On OMS inconsistency, quarantine stream and halt new orders

validation_contract.rule: Idempotent fills; out-of-order handling per policy; stable final state

failure_contract.rule: On OMS inconsistency, quarantine stream and halt new orders

S20.step_name: Classify trade close and compute canonical PnL
S20.validation: Close reason enum; deterministic PnL rounding rules
S20.failure: If PnL cannot be computed, emit TradeClosed with COMPUTATION_ERROR flag

validation_contract.rule: Close reason enum; deterministic PnL rounding rules

failure_contract.rule: If PnL cannot be computed, emit TradeClosed with COMPUTATION_ERROR flag

S21.validation: Only enumerated buckets; stable bucket mapping
S21.failure: If mapping fails, emit UNKNOWN bucket and quarantine for review

validation_contract.rule: Only enumerated buckets; stable bucket mapping

failure_contract.rule: If mapping fails, emit UNKNOWN bucket and quarantine for review

S22.failure: Emit proximity error; default to MOST_CONSERVATIVE proximity bucket

validation_contract.rule: Frozen-time supported; thresholds validated (no overlaps/gaps)

failure_contract.rule: Emit proximity error; default to MOST_CONSERVATIVE proximity bucket

S23.validation: Deterministic fallback hierarchy; matrix version pinned
S23.failure: If matrix missing, emit MatrixFallbackUsed + safest MatrixDecision

validation_contract.rule: Deterministic fallback hierarchy; matrix version pinned

failure_contract.rule: If matrix missing, emit MatrixFallbackUsed + safest MatrixDecision

S24.validation: Hard cap: no reentry beyond R2; no circular chains

validation_contract.rule: Hard cap: no reentry beyond R2; no circular chains

failure_contract.rule: Emit ReentrySuppressed with deterministic reason

S25.step_name: Loop: reentry intent follows same risk->order->route->transport->execute chain
S25.validation: Chain depth < configured max; velocity limits; idempotency keys enforced
S25.failure: On loop prevention trigger, enforce terminal cooldown and stop

validation_contract.rule: Chain depth < configured max; velocity limits; idempotency keys enforced

failure_contract.rule: On loop prevention trigger, enforce terminal cooldown and stop

S26.failure: If health is unknown, default to risk-off posture (suppress new intents)

validation_contract.rule: Deterministic thresholds; stable report format

failure_contract.rule: If health is unknown, default to risk-off posture (suppress new intents)

do_not_rebuild: The calendar-ingestor service already provides calendar event ingestion.
The common service already provides shared base classes, startup_health_gate, baseservice.
The compliance-monitor service already provides only a requirements.txt.
The dashboard-backend service already provides fastapi backend + react frontend dashboard.

data-ingestor.purpose: Normalizes MT4/DDE broker feeds into PriceTick events

capabilities.what_it_does: Normalizes MT4/DDE broker feeds into PriceTick events
do_not_rebuild: The data-ingestor service already provides normalizes mt4/dde broker feeds into pricetick events.
The data-validator service already provides minimal source files.

desktop-ui.purpose: Tkinter GUI, MQ4 scripts, indicator overlays

capabilities.what_it_does: Tkinter GUI, MQ4 scripts, indicator overlays
do_not_rebuild: The desktop-ui service already provides tkinter gui, mq4 scripts, indicator overlays.
The event-gateway service already provides event broker/gateway.
The execution-engine service already provides oms, trade outcome classification.
The flow-monitor service already provides workflow monitoring.
The flow-orchestrator service already provides service workflow coordination.
The gui-gateway service already provides gui communication gateway.
The indicator-engine service already provides currency strength, advanced indicators.
The reentry-engine service already provides processes traderesult into reentrydecision events.
The reentry-matrix-svc service already provides re-entry decision matrix.
The reporter service already provides report generation/publishing.
The risk-manager service already provides risk management and mitigation.

scripts.purpose: Shell scripts for enterprise service integration/validation

capabilities.what_it_does: Shell scripts for enterprise service integration/validation
do_not_rebuild: The scripts service already provides shell scripts for enterprise service integration/validation.
The signal-generator service already provides signal processing from indicators.
The telemetry-daemon service already provides system-wide health monitoring, alerting, observability.

transport-router.purpose: Event routing and validation between services

capabilities.what_it_does: Event routing and validation between services
do_not_rebuild: The transport-router service already provides event routing and validation between services.

UI-PRODUCT-DESKTOP-OPERATOR.name: Desktop Operator UI
UI-PRODUCT-DESKTOP-OPERATOR.purpose: Provide live trading visibility, signal review, configuration, safety intervention, history, analytics, diagnostics, and reentry controls.

UI-PRODUCT-DASHBOARD-BACKEND.name: Dashboard Backend
UI-PRODUCT-DASHBOARD-BACKEND.purpose: Aggregate trading, market, signal, position, calendar, metric, and audit data into UI-friendly REST and streaming responses.

UI-PRODUCT-GUI-GATEWAY.name: GUI Gateway
UI-PRODUCT-GUI-GATEWAY.purpose: Expose operator API and real-time UI subscriptions for system status, dashboard summaries, positions, signals, orders, calendar events, and alerts.

UI-PRODUCT-MT4-OVERLAY.name: MT4 Expiry Zones Overlay
UI-PRODUCT-MT4-OVERLAY.purpose: Display expiry zones, indicator summaries, spot data, and MT4-facing UI data served over local HTTP.

UI-PRODUCT-GRAFANA-OPS.name: Trading System Overview Dashboard
UI-PRODUCT-GRAFANA-OPS.purpose: Provide operations-oriented monitoring distinct from the operator trading UI.

UI-SCREEN-LIVE-TRADING.name: Live Trading
UI-SCREEN-LIVE-TRADING.purpose: Show real-time trading state and allow low-risk live workflow actions.

UI-SCREEN-CONFIGURATION.name: Configuration
UI-SCREEN-CONFIGURATION.purpose: Manage global communication settings, indicator parameters, strategy settings, and dependency guardrails.

UI-SCREEN-SIGNALS-HISTORY.name: Signals History
UI-SCREEN-SIGNALS-HISTORY.purpose: Review historical and current signals with filters, drill-down, and traceability.

UI-SCREEN-TRADE-HISTORY.name: Trade History
UI-SCREEN-TRADE-HISTORY.purpose: Inspect completed and in-flight trades, execution results, outcomes, and PnL reconciliation.

UI-SCREEN-HISTORY-ANALYTICS.name: History and Analytics
UI-SCREEN-HISTORY-ANALYTICS.purpose: Provide logs, KPIs, analytics views, and operator review workflows.

UI-SCREEN-DDE-PRICE-FEED.name: DDE Price Feed
UI-SCREEN-DDE-PRICE-FEED.purpose: Monitor and operate MT4 DDE price ingestion and subscriptions.

UI-SCREEN-ECONOMIC-CALENDAR.name: Economic Calendar
UI-SCREEN-ECONOMIC-CALENDAR.purpose: Monitor calendar ingestion, anticipation states, integrity checks, and calendar import safety controls.

UI-SCREEN-SYSTEM-STATUS.name: System Status
UI-SCREEN-SYSTEM-STATUS.purpose: Show health, incidents, resources, degraded states, and admin-gated operational tools.

UI-SCREEN-REENTRY-MATRIX.name: Reentry Matrix
UI-SCREEN-REENTRY-MATRIX.purpose: Inspect and manage reentry decision matrix dimensions, cells, versions, overrides, and decision traceability.

UI-SCREEN-REENTRY-PARAMETER-SETS.name: Reentry Parameter Sets
UI-SCREEN-REENTRY-PARAMETER-SETS.purpose: Manage typed parameter sets for reentry behavior and link active sets to matrix profiles.

UI-SCREEN-ALERTS.name: Alerts and Notifications

UI-API-DASHBOARD-POSITIONS.purpose: Open positions for live trading and risk views.

UI-API-GUI-SYSTEM-STATUS.purpose: Overall system status and service health.

source_authority_rule.issue: Not all files have equal authority; canonical structured JSON and active governance baselines win over narrative notes, PDFs, generated reports, and implementation samples.
source_authority_rule.resolution: Apply eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json first, then use domain canonical files.

module_identity_and_count_drift.issue: Older summaries reference 28 modules or symbolic-only IDs, while the current module_catalog.json contains 27 modules with numeric IDs and canonical symbols.
module_identity_and_count_drift.resolution: Use module_catalog.json for current canonical identity. Use older files only to explain drift.

atonic_vs_canonical_process_drift.issue: ATONIC lifecycle markdown decomposes the system into 71 atomic operations across 11 phases; canonical JSON uses 26 module-handoff steps across 10 phases.
atonic_vs_canonical_process_drift.resolution: Use process_step_catalog.json and updated_trading_process_aligned.json for current canonical process. Use ATONIC and crosswalk files for granular runbook detail.

file_mapping_incompleteness.issue: file_module_mapping.csv is incomplete and contains unassigned rows/stale symbols according to prior summaries and reconciliation notes.
file_mapping_incompleteness.resolution: Use file mapping as physical implementation evidence only. Do not let it override canonical module/process catalogs.

sample_ea_low_authority.issue: HUEY_P_EA_ExecutionEngine_8.mql4 is a sample moving-average crossover EA and not the full documented EAFIX execution engine.
sample_ea_low_authority.resolution: Use it only for MQL4 syntax/example patterns. Do not infer canonical execution behavior from it.

calendar_reference_availability_gap.issue: Several calendar PDFs and specs are known from repo summaries but are not all present in current /mnt/data.
affected_files: Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf
Economic Calendar Subsystem - Technical Analysis & Documentation.pdf
Enhanced Economic Calendar System - Complete Implementation.pdf
calendar_reference_availability_gap.resolution: If missing locally, report missing reference before implementing calendar changes; use available canonical process/module references as fallback only for routing, not detailed calendar design.

steps: Open module_index entry if module symbol is known.
If only source file is known, check source_code_file_index then module_catalog.json.
Use file_module_mapping.csv only as physical evidence.

lookup_recipes.question_pattern: What file should AI open for a contract?
What files implement an MT4/Python channel?
steps: Use MT4_Python communication channels.txt for narrative/file discovery.
Use MT4 authoritative reference for platform constraints.
Apply ui_catalog authority and safety rules.
Then map services/files through service_index and source_code_file_index.

lookup_recipes.question_pattern: Can we reuse an existing service instead of building custom?
steps: Then check module/service mapping before planning new code.
Open the authoritative canonical file and only then consult legacy/explanatory references.

missing_or_unavailable_sources.summary_short: Authoritative deterministic design rules for economic calendar architecture, CAL8/CAL5/RCI identity, state machine, debounce, single-writer authority, proximity, and anti-pattern guards.
open_when: Question asks about economic calendar, CAL8, CAL5, RCI, ForexFactory ingestion, proximity, calendar CSV, event state machine, debounce, file_seq, or checksum.
companion_files: Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf

missing_or_unavailable_sources.summary_short: Authority registry assigning document classifications, authority levels, mine_complete status, superseded-by relationships, and conflict-use notes.
Contract-boundary registry mapping modules to input/output types, required implementation/test/schema file patterns, and output-contract validation rules.
Small generated list of module IDs extracted from the process document, with count, source, and timestamp.
Current repo file registry listing file IDs, paths, directory IDs, extensions, module assignments, process steps, component IDs, verification time, and scope class.
Historical Python file inventory with full paths, sizes, and last-modified dates; superseded by file_registry.csv.
Utility script that parses the process YAML, extracts unique module IDs, and writes modules_extracted.json.
Automation Lifecycle Verification Tooling documentation covering static integrity, graph connectivity, planned determinism/sandbox/negative/idempotency layers, and trigger contracts.
MVP calendar subsystem spec for single-source ForexFactory CSV ingestion, normalization, impact filtering, pre/post trade-block windows, per-symbol context, and CSV export.
Report analysing module responsibilities, dependencies, simplification opportunities, and accuracy issues across the HUEY_P/EAFIX system.

missing_or_unavailable_sources.file_name: Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf
missing_or_unavailable_sources.repo_path_hint: EAFIX_auth_docs/05_calendar_system/Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf
missing_or_unavailable_sources.summary_short: Implementation-ready plan for ForexFactory auto-download, real-time proximity, MT4 CSV bridge completion, error recovery, and circuit breakers.

missing_or_unavailable_sources.file_name: Economic Calendar Subsystem - Technical Analysis & Documentation.pdf
missing_or_unavailable_sources.summary_short: Production-ready analysis of the economic calendar subsystem: import engine, processing pipeline, event enhancement, trigger engine, database, integration, testing, monitoring.
Enhanced calendar configuration/deployment reference covering import schedule, retries, processing limits, trigger offsets, parameter sets, database, notifications, and risk/portfolio settings.

missing_or_unavailable_sources.file_name: Enhanced Economic Calendar System - Complete Implementation.pdf
missing_or_unavailable_sources.summary_short: Comprehensive Python implementation sketch for enhanced calendar database, downloads, processing, triggering, risk, notifications, and monitoring.

AI-oriented module-centric repository architecture guide covering module-boundary organization, co-located artifacts, ID-based organization, incremental migration, registry abstraction, deterministic execution, and anti-patterns.
Services directory overview summarizing service naming conventions, numeric ID-prefixed files, plugin architecture, service inventory, and high-level service flow.
Draft source-grounded process spec that separates canonical process flow, modular Python runtime, and HUEY_P/MT4 bridge realities; identifies confirmed artifacts and drift.
Longer source-grounded process specification with evidence tables, confirmed runtime artifacts, planned/conceptual artifacts, and source conflicts.
Pytest scaffold containing skipped test shells for infrastructure, ID/rename, parser/descriptor, registry writer, watcher, reconciliation, and integration phases.
Mined active standards registry with language/project rules, enforcement gates, superseded rules, and needs-review items; not present in current /mnt/data.
Submodule documentation checklist for selected modules and required contract documents; not present in current /mnt/data.
Minimal reusable-pattern YAML stub with empty intent, inputs, outputs, steps, and validation sections; placeholder only.
Plain-language onboarding explanation of the EAFIX/HUEY_P system using analogies and flow walkthroughs; no authority over canonical catalogs.
Module catalog JSON schema reference; use only when validating the corresponding catalog identity model and check for symbolic-vs-numeric drift.
Symbol-to-numeric ID map mentioned by doc_authority; not present in current /mnt/data.
Identity-migration execution scaffold mentioned by doc_authority; not present in current /mnt/data.
Migration completion record mentioned by doc_authority; not present in current /mnt/data.
Runnable migration validation gate commands mentioned by doc_authority; not present in current /mnt/data.

update_when: A new project knowledge file is added or removed.
A conflict or drift issue is resolved.
validation_checks: Every source_files record must have file_name, status, authority_level, summary_short, authoritative_for, not_authoritative_for, open_when, companion_files.
Every index reference should point to a known file_name or explicitly be marked missing/external.
Current /mnt/data file SHA256 values should be regenerated on update.
